Class GameEngineTest

java.lang.Object
jogo.engine.GameEngineTest
class GameEngineTest extends Object
Test class for the GameEngine class. This class tests the main game actions managed by GameEngine, such as creating structures, upgrading structures, searching resources and retrieving structure information from the map. The tests verify both successful actions and expected exceptions when invalid actions are performed.
Author:
Fabio Cruz, Tiago Silva

  • Constructor Details

    • GameEngineTest

      GameEngineTest()

  • Method Details

    • setUp

      @BeforeEach void setUp()
      Creates a new map and a new player before each test. This ensures that every test starts with clean objects and that previous tests do not affect the next ones.

    • testSuccessCreateStructure

      @Test void testSuccessCreateStructure() throws GameException
      Tests if a structure can be created successfully. The player receives enough resources and Action Points before the test. After creating the structure, the test verifies if the map position becomes occupied and if the correct resources and Action Points were removed from the player.
      Throws:
      GameException - if an unexpected game error occurs

    • testCreateStructureThrowsCoordinatesOutOfBoundException

      @Test void testCreateStructureThrowsCoordinatesOutOfBoundException()
      Tests if creating a structure outside the map boundaries throws CoordinatesOutOfBoundsException. The test checks both negative coordinates and coordinates equal to or greater than the map size.

    • testCreateStructureThrowsSpaceAlreadyOccupiedException

      @Test void testCreateStructureThrowsSpaceAlreadyOccupiedException() throws GameException
      Tests if creating a structure in an occupied position throws SpaceAlreadyOccupiedException.
      Throws:
      GameException - if an unexpected game error occurs while preparing the test

    • testCreateStructureThrowsInsufficientResourcesException

      @Test void testCreateStructureThrowsInsufficientResourcesException()
      Tests if creating a structure without enough resources throws InsufficientResourcesException. The player only receives Action Points, but no construction materials.

    • testCreateStructureThrowsInsufficientAPException

      @Test void testCreateStructureThrowsInsufficientAPException()
      Tests if creating a structure without enough Action Points throws InsufficientAPException. The player receives construction materials but does not receive enough Action Points.

    • testSuccessUpgradeStructure

      @Test void testSuccessUpgradeStructure() throws GameException
      Tests if a structure can be upgraded successfully. The test verifies if the upgrade consumes the correct resources, consumes the correct Action Points and increases the structure level.
      Throws:
      GameException - if an unexpected game error occurs

    • testUpgradeStructureThrowsCoordinatesOutOfBoundException

      @Test void testUpgradeStructureThrowsCoordinatesOutOfBoundException()
      Tests if upgrading a structure outside the map boundaries throws CoordinatesOutOfBoundsException.

    • testUpgradeStructureThrowInsufficientAPException

      @Test void testUpgradeStructureThrowInsufficientAPException() throws GameException
      Tests if upgrading a structure without enough Action Points throws InsufficientAPException.
      Throws:
      GameException - if an unexpected game error occurs while preparing the test

    • testUpgradeStructureThrowInsufficientResourcesException

      @Test void testUpgradeStructureThrowInsufficientResourcesException() throws GameException
      Tests if upgrading a structure without enough upgrade resources throws InsufficientResourcesException.
      Throws:
      GameException - if an unexpected game error occurs while preparing the test

    • testUpgradeStructureThrowsStructureDontExistException

      @Test void testUpgradeStructureThrowsStructureDontExistException()
      Tests if upgrading a position without a structure throws StructureDontExistException.

    • testUpgradeStructureThrowsUnauthorizedActionException

      @Test void testUpgradeStructureThrowsUnauthorizedActionException() throws GameException
      Tests if a player cannot upgrade a structure that belongs to another player. Expected result: UnauthorizedActionException is thrown.
      Throws:
      GameException - if an unexpected game error occurs while preparing the test

    • testUpgradeStructureIncreaseStats

      @Test void testUpgradeStructureIncreaseStats() throws GameException
      Tests if upgrading a structure increases its statistics correctly. The test verifies if the expense and profit values are updated according to the Forest upgrade values.
      Throws:
      GameException - if an unexpected game error occurs

    • testSearchResourcesSuccess

      @Test void testSearchResourcesSuccess()
      Tests if searching resources works correctly. The test verifies if the player loses the correct amount of Action Points and receives a resource quantity inside the expected random interval.
      Throws:
      GameException - if an unexpected game error occurs

    • testSearchResourcesSuccessRepeatedly

      @Test void testSearchResourcesSuccessRepeatedly() throws GameException
      Tests the searchResources method multiple times. This test ensures that the random quantity of resources obtained always stays inside the expected interval.
      Throws:
      GameException - if an unexpected game error occurs

    • testSearchResourcesThrowsInsufficientAPException

      @Test void testSearchResourcesThrowsInsufficientAPException()
      Tests if searching resources without enough Action Points throws InsufficientAPException.

    • testSearchResourcesThrowsGameExceptionForInvalidType

      @Test void testSearchResourcesThrowsGameExceptionForInvalidType()
      Tests if searching for an invalid resource type throws GameException. Invalid resource types are NONE and ACTION_POINTS. The test also verifies if the exception message is correct.

    • testGetStructureInfoSuccess

      @Test void testGetStructureInfoSuccess() throws GameException
      Tests if getStructureInfo returns the correct structure. The test creates a Forest structure and then verifies if the returned structure is not null, has the correct type and belongs to the correct player.
      Throws:
      GameException - if an unexpected game error occurs

    • testGetStructureInfoThrowsCoordinatesOutOfBoundException

      @Test void testGetStructureInfoThrowsCoordinatesOutOfBoundException()
      Tests if getting structure information outside the map boundaries throws CoordinatesOutOfBoundsException.

    • testGetStructureInfoThrowsExceptionWhenEmpty

      @Test void testGetStructureInfoThrowsExceptionWhenEmpty() throws GameException
      Tests if getting structure information from an empty position throws GameException. The test also verifies if the exception message is correct.
      Throws:
      GameException
